body: what is UMA?

If/when this disputes, UMA voters examining the record will find:

Trump unilateral announcement (clearly unilateral per his own wording)
Pakistan PM thank-you tweet (third-party mediator, not Iran government)
NYT framing as Pakistan "courting" and "seeking to mediate" (not validating)
Iran officials publicly rejecting (Ghalibaf adviser, Araghchi, negotiating team)
Press TV / Iran International / Tehran Times reporting rejection
Blockade continuing (a stated Iran claim of ceasefire violation)
No joint statement exists
Iran refusing Islamabad talks as of 21:07 GMT+1
This is a strong NO case on rule-text.

Agreed, strongly agreed. Rules are clear as a day; it has to be the same playbook as two weeks ago to qualify (which it clearly isn't). However, there is big money to be lost on the yes side, and these ppl won't give up easily, likely disputing the outcome if NO or proposing yes otherwise.

No, there was a unilateral extension by Trump only. Learn to read

Based on conversations with Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Field Marshal Asim Munir, of Pakistan, and wherein they requested that I hold off the destructive force being sent tonight to Iran, and subject to the Islamic Republic of Iran agreeing to the COMPLETE, IMMEDIATE, and SAFE OPENING of the Strait of Hormuz, I agree to suspend the bombing and attack of Iran for a period of two weeks. This will be a double sided CEASEFIRE! The reason for doing so is that we have already met and exceeded all Military objectives, and are very far along with a definitive Agreement concerning Longterm PEACE with Iran, and PEACE in the Middle East. We received a 10 point proposal from Iran, and believe it is a workable basis on which to negotiate. Almost all of the various points of past contention have been agreed to between the United States and Iran, but a two week period will allow the Agreement to be finalized and consummated. On behalf of the United States of America, as President, and also representing the Countries of the Middle East, it is an Honor to have this Longterm problem close to resolution. Thank you for your attention to this matter! President DONALD J. TRUMP

🔑 Key Point for YES

The most important rule here is:

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, the market resolves to YES — regardless of what happens afterward.

This is the decisive factor.

In this case, both sides have already confirmed the extension before the deadline:

* Donald Trump publicly stated the ceasefire is being extended
* Esmaeil Baghaei officially confirmed the extension on state television

That alone satisfies the requirement of a qualifying agreement reached in time.

👉 What happens after that—violations, tensions, or even collapse of the ceasefire—is irrelevant to resolution.

This is the golden point many are missing:
The agreement was acknowledged by both parties before the deadline. That is sufficient.

👉 Therefore, under the rules, this market should resolve to YES.

IRIB (Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting)
